title: Medullary thyroid carcinoma: amyloid (Congo red) stroma
vignette: A 30-year-old man comes to the office with a neck lump. The patient is otherwise asymptomatic and discovered the nodule incidentally while showering. His medical history is significant for a recently diagnosed pheochromocytoma, which was successfully removed. The patient's father died of thyroid cancer in his 30s. An ultrasound reveals a hypoechoic 3-cm nodule in the right lobe of the thyroid gland. Fine-needle biopsy of the nodule is consistent with a subtype of thyroid cancer. The patient undergoes total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection. Which of the following is the most likely histological finding?

clues.note: Pheochromocytoma plus thyroid cancer in a young patient is the MEN2 pattern. MEN2A/2B feature medullary thyroid carcinoma + pheochromocytoma (RET proto-oncogene), steering toward medullary, not papillary, cancer.

clues.text: father died of thyroid cancer in his 30s
clues.note: A family history of young-onset thyroid cancer indicates an autosomal dominant familial syndrome (MEN2/RET). Hereditary medullary thyroid carcinoma is the unifying diagnosis.

The 'subtype' in this MEN2 context is medullary thyroid carcinoma, arising from parafollicular C cells (calcitonin-secreting). Its histology shows nests of polygonal/spindled cells in an amyloid stroma.

whatMatters: Pheochromocytoma + familial young-onset thyroid cancer = MEN2 with medullary thyroid carcinoma (C-cell origin, RET). Its histology is nests of polygonal cells in a stroma containing amyloid that stains with Congo red - answer C.
strategy: Use the MEN2 trigger (pheochromocytoma + family history) to pick MEDULLARY thyroid carcinoma, then recall its signature histology: sheets/nests of polygonal C cells with AMYLOID (calcitonin-derived) stroma that is Congo red-positive and apple-green birefringent. Distinguish from papillary (psammoma bodies, Orphan-Annie nuclei) and follicular/anaplastic.

options.text: Branching structures with interspersed calcified bodies
Follicular hyperplasia with tall cells forming intrafollicular projections
Nests of polygonal cells with Congo red-positive deposits
Pleomorphic giant cell nests with occasional multinucleated cells
Sheets of uniform cells forming small follicles

eliminations.label: papillary structures + psammoma bodies - papillary carcinoma
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: branching papillae with calcified psammoma bodies = papillary thyroid carcinoma. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: papillary cancer is not the MEN2-associated tumor; the syndrome points to medullary (amyloid), not papillary. ✅ Correct if: a sporadic thyroid nodule with Orphan-Annie nuclei, nuclear grooves, and psammoma bodies.

eliminations.label: tall cells with papillary projections - papillary variant
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: follicular epithelium with tall cells/intrafollicular projections suggests a papillary (tall-cell variant) pattern. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: that is papillary-type, not medullary; the MEN2/pheochromocytoma context demands medullary histology. You chose this. ✅ Correct if: papillary carcinoma features dominated.

eliminations.label: pleomorphic giant cells - anaplastic carcinoma
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: pleomorphic, multinucleated giant cells = anaplastic (undifferentiated) thyroid carcinoma. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: anaplastic occurs in the elderly with rapid growth/invasion, not a young MEN2 patient. ✅ Correct if: an older patient with a rapidly enlarging, fixed thyroid mass.

eliminations.label: uniform cells forming small follicles - follicular carcinoma/adenoma
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: uniform follicular cells forming microfollicles = follicular neoplasm. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: follicular carcinoma is not MEN2-associated and lacks amyloid; the C-cell amyloid pattern is expected here. ✅ Correct if: capsular/vascular invasion of a follicular-patterned tumor.

reasoning: MEN2 thyroid tumor = medullary thyroid carcinoma (parafollicular C cells)
C cells secrete calcitonin that deposits as amyloid in the stroma
Nests of polygonal cells with Congo red-positive amyloid -> answer C
mechanism: Medullary thyroid carcinoma arises from the parafollicular C cells of the thyroid, which secrete calcitonin. In MEN2A/2B (germline RET proto-oncogene mutations) it co-occurs with pheochromocytoma (and parathyroid hyperplasia in 2A or mucosal neuromas/marfanoid habitus in 2B). Histologically, the tumor shows nests and sheets of polygonal-to-spindled C cells separated by a stroma containing AMYLOID derived from deposited calcitonin; this amyloid stains red with Congo red and shows apple-green birefringence under polarized light. This differs from papillary (psammoma bodies, Orphan-Annie nuclei), follicular (microfollicles, capsular/vascular invasion), and anaplastic (pleomorphic giant cells) carcinomas. Prophylactic thyroidectomy is offered to RET carriers.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Endocrine/Pathology > Thyroid cancer & MEN2: medullary carcinoma (C cells, calcitonin, amyloid/Congo red, RET, MEN2A/2B with pheochromocytoma); papillary (psammoma bodies, Orphan-Annie nuclei), follicular (invasion), anaplastic (giant cells).

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The syndrome (pheo + familial thyroid cancer) selects the tumor (medullary); then recall its unique amyloid histology. Don't default to papillary just because it's the most common thyroid cancer overall.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on medullary thyroid carcinoma, parafollicular C cells, calcitonin amyloid, Congo red/apple-green birefringence, MEN2/RET, psammoma bodies? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: You chose B (tall cells/papillary projections) - that's a papillary pattern. The MEN2 context forces MEDULLARY carcinoma with amyloid (Congo red), answer C.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Anchor: pheochromocytoma + thyroid cancer = MEN2 medullary = AMYLOID (Congo red).

keywords.back: Nests of polygonal C cells in amyloid stroma (Congo red+, apple-green birefringence)

keywords.front: Cell of origin / hormone in medullary carcinoma

keywords.back: Psammoma bodies, Orphan-Annie nuclei, nuclear grooves
2A: medullary + pheo + parathyroid hyperplasia; 2B: medullary + pheo + mucosal neuromas/marfanoid
Elderly, pleomorphic giant cells, rapidly enlarging, poor prognosis

firstAid.detail: Endocrine/Path: C cells, calcitonin, amyloid stroma, RET, MEN2.
firstAid.location: Endocrine/Path: C cells, calcitonin, amyloid stroma, RET, MEN2.

firstAid.detail: Endo: RET proto-oncogene, pheochromocytoma, prophylactic thyroidectomy.
firstAid.location: Endo: RET proto-oncogene, pheochromocytoma, prophylactic thyroidectomy.

firstAid.detail: Path: papillary, follicular, anaplastic distinguishing features.
firstAid.location: Path: papillary, follicular, anaplastic distinguishing features.

memoryHook: Pheo + family thyroid cancer = MEN2 medullary. C cells dump calcitonin -> AMYLOID nests (Congo red turns it red).
